MED/LGR A/C HAD AT LEAST 24 BIRD STRIKES ON T/O.  20-30 BIRDS  WERE SEEN. THERE WERE SEVERAL RAPID IMPACT SOUNDS & A MOMENTARY  SLT HESITATION IN ACCELERATION. BOTH SETS OF ENG INSTS APPRD  NORMAL THRUOUT FLT. A STRONG BURNING BIRD SMELL BEGAN IMMEDIATELY  AND LASTED 5-10 MINS. ADVSD TWR OF STRIKES. CONTD TO IND BECAUSE  GAUGES WERE NORMAL.  FLT WAS UNEVENTFUL.  2 DOZ BIRDS WERE FOUND  ON RWY. 3-4 STRIKE MARKS IN #2 ENG, 2-3 IN #1 & SEVERAL OTHERS ON  FUSELAGE & WINGS. ENG #1 HAD 3 DAMAGED BLADES, INCLUDING 1  MISSING PIECE (1/2" X 1"), ENG WILL BE CHANGED. SAMPLE OF  FEATHERS WAS SAVED. PERHAPS IF ARPT VEHICLE COULD HAVE PRECEDED  US DOWN RWY IN AN ATTEMPT TO FLUSH THE FLOCK THE STRIKE COULD  HAVE BEEN AVOIDED.

CORP A/C REPTD A BIRD STRIKE TO WINDSHLD SHORTLY AFTER T/O. PLT  ELECTED TO CONT FLT. BIRDS ARE A MAJOR PROBLEM AT THIS ARPT. THE  WORST TIME IS MIGRATION, ALTHO THERE ARE BIRDS YEAR ROUND.  CANNONS WERE INSTALLED 3 YRS AGO BUT HAVE NOT BEEN ACTIVE LATELY  AND HAVE LITTLE EFFECT AT BEST. ARPT SEND VEHICLE W/ SHOTGUN OR  FLARE GUN TO SCARE BIRDS IF WE CALL.  THIS USUALLY HAS LITTLE  EFFECT. LOW SPOTS FILL W/ WATER & ATTRACT BIRDS. OFTEN BIRDS ARE  SCARED UP INTO THE PATH OF A/C LANDING OR TAKING OFF. SOMETHING  NEEDS TO BE DONE BEFORE A BIRD STRIKE RESULTS IN A MAJOR  ACCIDENT.
